verdict: All 8 checks pass. Reading is well-scoped, faithfully derived from corpus, beginner-accessible with consistent bold-term definitions and concrete-first structure, within length budget (1530w / 7 min), primary citation present, no frontmatter, correct six-section template, no assessment references, Judgment Framework correctly deferred.

format.notes: All six required sections present in correct order. No YAML frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json parses cleanly.

citations.notes: [1] IBM AI Bias used inline throughout. [2] IBM Data Bias. [3] Navigli et al. arXiv 2023. Bibliography matches all inline markers.

no_forward_references.notes: References to 9.3 and 9.5 are valid back-references established in corpus prerequisites. Reference to Topics 9.7-9.9 for the Judgment Framework is a single named-and-deferred sentence with no teaching of forward content — compliant with scope rule.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus sections 5.1-5.7. Camera analogy, sentiment labelling example, loan/zip-code example, facial recognition example, predictive policing example, and cognitive bias layer commentary all have verbatim or close paraphrase matches in corpus.

beginner_readability.notes: Encoding, Training data, Labelling bias, Historical bias, Selection bias, Representation bias, Feedback loop amplification all defined on first use with bold-term format. Confirmation bias and Automation bias back-referenced to 9.3 and 9.5 respectively. Concrete examples precede or accompany abstractions throughout. No hedging language detected. Sentence length appropriate for beginner audience.

formatting.notes: Feedback loop steps rendered as numbered list (lines 40-45). In Practice section uses a table. Key Takeaways use bullet list. All Key Concepts sections open with a bold-term definition. No paragraphs exceed 6 sentences. Inline examples present in Overview, Key Concepts (each sub-section), and In Practice.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear anywhere in the reading.

scope.judgment_framework_handling: bare name + defer only — one sentence at In Practice close: 'A structured approach...is introduced in Topics 9.7-9.9.'
scope.aggregation_bias_handling: Term appears briefly in Worked Example checklist and In Practice table as a named check item, not taught as a standalone concept — appropriate minimal surface without teaching beyond headline.
scope.notes: Reading stays within the headline 'How human biases get encoded into AI training data'. Four encoding mechanisms (labelling, historical, selection, feedback loop) are the named sub-concepts from the artifact plan. Judgment Framework correctly deferred.
